summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a classic ’72 show featuring Jerry’s white Strat tone, Phil’s Truckin’ bass solo and Bill Graham’s iconic introduction.
review: The recording is praised for its crystal‑clear Betty Cantor‑Jackson soundboard mix, delivering the classic 1972 Dead vibe. The performance shines with Jerry’s rich Strat guitar work, a standout bass solo from Phil in “Truckin’”, and an unforgettable Bill Graham intro, while minor flubs are quickly smoothed over.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews overwhelmingly highlight superb performance and excellent soundboard quality; weighted rating (70% show, 30% recording) yields a high score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Betty Cantor‑Jackson’s mix is described as crystal‑clear and superbly balanced; no significant audio flaws are mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Classic ’72 set with tight transitions; after a brief flub the band tightened up, highlighted by Phil’s bass solo and Jerry’s added lick on “Bertha”.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His white Strat is praised for its rich‑raunchy tone and he adds a tasteful lick to the end of “Bertha” after a flub.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass solo out of “Truckin’” is highlighted as a must‑hear moment.
